classes.name: Acrisols
classes.description: Strongly weathered, acidic tropical and subtropical soils with clay accumulation in the subsoil and generally low natural fertility.

classes.name: Albeluvisols
classes.description: Cool-climate forest soils with bleached upper horizons over clay-enriched subsoil, reflecting eluviation and lessivage processes.

classes.name: Alisols
classes.description: Acid, clay-rich soils with high exchangeable aluminum and pronounced subsoil clay increase, common in humid warm regions.

classes.name: Andosols
classes.description: Volcanic-ash-derived soils with high porosity, high organic matter stabilization, and strong phosphorus fixation.

classes.name: Arenosols
classes.description: Very sandy soils with weak profile development, rapid drainage, and typically low nutrient and water holding capacity.

classes.name: Calcisols
classes.description: Dryland soils characterized by secondary calcium carbonate accumulation in subsoil horizons.

classes.name: Cambisols
classes.description: Young to moderately developed soils showing early horizon differentiation but lacking strong diagnostic accumulations.

classes.name: Chernozems
classes.description: Dark, humus-rich steppe soils with high base saturation and high natural fertility, typically under grassland climates.

classes.name: Cryosols
classes.description: Cold-region soils influenced by permafrost and cryoturbation, with shallow active layers and strong freeze-thaw effects.

classes.name: Durisols
classes.description: Arid-zone soils containing cemented silica-rich duripan horizons that restrict rooting and water movement.

classes.name: Ferralsols
classes.description: Deeply weathered tropical soils dominated by iron and aluminum oxides, with stable structure but low nutrient reserves.

classes.name: Fluvisols
classes.description: Recent alluvial soils on floodplains and deltas, often stratified and periodically renewed by fluvial sediment deposition.

classes.name: Gleysols
classes.description: Waterlogged soils showing reducing conditions and gley features due to prolonged saturation or shallow groundwater.

classes.name: Gypsisols
classes.description: Arid and semi-arid soils with substantial secondary gypsum accumulation in subsurface horizons.

classes.name: Histosols
classes.description: Organic soils formed from peat accumulation in wetlands, with very high organic carbon and low bulk density.

classes.name: Kastanozems
classes.description: Steppe soils with dark humus-rich topsoils, high base status, and moderate organic matter compared with Chernozems.

classes.name: Leptosols
classes.description: Very shallow or extremely stony soils over hard rock, common on steep slopes and eroding uplands.

classes.name: Lixisols
classes.description: Warm-region soils with clay accumulation and low-activity clays, generally less fertile than Luvisols.

classes.name: Luvisols
classes.description: Soils with marked clay translocation into subsoil horizons and relatively high base saturation, often productive under management.

classes.name: Nitisols
classes.description: Deep, red, well-structured tropical soils with strong aggregate stability and relatively good physical properties.

classes.name: Phaeozems
classes.description: Humus-rich temperate grassland and forest-steppe soils with high base status and generally high agricultural potential.

classes.name: Planosols
classes.description: Soils with abrupt textural contrast and slowly permeable subsoil, often causing seasonal perched water and surface wetness.

classes.name: Plinthosols
classes.description: Tropical soils containing plinthite or iron-rich nodules that can harden irreversibly upon repeated wetting and drying.

classes.name: Podzols
classes.description: Acid, sandy, leached soils with organic and iron/aluminum accumulation in subsoil, typical under coniferous or heath vegetation.

classes.name: Regosols
classes.description: Weakly developed mineral soils in unconsolidated materials, often in erosional, depositional, or geologically young landscapes.

classes.name: Solonchaks
classes.description: Strongly saline soils with high soluble salt contents, common in arid basins, coastal flats, and poorly drained depressions.

classes.name: Solonetz
classes.description: Sodic soils with high exchangeable sodium and dense columnar or prismatic subsoil structure, often limiting infiltration and rooting.

classes.name: Stagnosols
classes.description: Seasonally waterlogged soils where perched water causes redox mottling and stagnic properties in upper profile layers.

classes.name: Umbrisols
classes.description: Acid soils with dark, organic-rich surface horizons and low base saturation, often in humid temperate to montane regions.

classes.name: Vertisols
classes.description: Clay-rich shrink-swell soils that crack deeply when dry and swell when wet, producing self-mixing and challenging engineering behavior.
